author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

originalBook: In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.
title: Chapter 21

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Chapter 21.—47.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "Accordingly, as we have said, the Lord Christ cried, ‘Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: It is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And he said, Who art Thou, Lord?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And the Lord said, I am Christ of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And he, trembling and astonished, said, Lord, what wilt Thou have me to do?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And so presently it goes on, ‘But Saul arose from the earth; and when his eyes were opened, he saw no man.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: See here how blindness, coming in punishment of madness, obscures the light in the eyes of the persecutor, not to be again expelled except by baptism!

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Let us see, therefore, what he did in the city.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: ‘Ananias,’ it is said, ‘entered into the house to Saul, and putting his hands on him, said, Brother Saul, the Lord, even Jesus, that appeared unto thee in the way as thou camest, hath sent me, that thou mightest receive thy sight, and be filled with the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And immediately there fell from his eyes as it had been scales; and he received sight forthwith, and arose, and was baptized.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Seeing therefore that Paul, being freed by baptism from the offense of persecution, received again his eyesight freed from guilt, why will not you, a persecutor and traditor, blinded by false baptism be baptized by those whom you persecute?"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: You do not prove that I, whom you wish to baptize afresh, am either a persecutor or a traditor.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And if you prove this charge against any one, yet the persecutor and traditor is not to be baptized afresh, if he had been baptized already with the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For the reason why it was necessary that Paul should be baptized was that he had never been washed in any baptism of the kind.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Therefore what you have chosen to insert about Paul has no point of resemblance with the case which you are arguing with us.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But if you had not inserted this, you would have found no place for your childish declamation, "See how blindness comes in punishment of madness, not to be again expelled except by baptism!"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For with how much more force might one exclaim against you, See how blindness comes in punishment of madness, which, finding its similitude in Simon, not in Paul, is not expelled from you even when you have received baptism?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For if persecutors ought to be baptized by those whom they persecute, then let Primianus be baptized by the followers of Maximianus, whom he persecuted with the utmost eagerness.
